name: Plus
description: Addition operator; also denotes positive sign or concatenation in some contexts.

name: Minus
description: Subtraction operator; also denotes negative sign or set difference in some notations.

name: Multiplication
description: Multiplication operator for scalars; also used for cross product of vectors.

name: Division
description: Division operator. In most mathematical writing, the fraction bar or slash is preferred.

name: Plus-Minus
description: Indicates that a quantity may be either added or subtracted. Used in error margins and the quadratic formula.

name: Equals
description: Denotes equality between two expressions.

name: Not Equal To
description: Denotes that two expressions are not equal.

name: Less Than
description: Strict inequality: the left side is less than the right side.

name: Greater Than
description: Strict inequality: the left side is greater than the right side.

name: Less Than or Equal To
description: Non-strict inequality: the left side is less than or equal to the right side.

name: Greater Than or Equal To
description: Non-strict inequality: the left side is greater than or equal to the right side.

name: Approximately Equal To
description: Indicates that two quantities are approximately equal, used when the exact value is not required or known.

name: Proportional To
description: Indicates that one quantity is proportional to another, i.e., y ∝ x means y = kx for some constant k.

name: Infinity
description: Represents an unbounded quantity; used in limits, series, and set theory to denote values without an upper (or lower) bound.

name: Square Root
description: The principal square root operator. √x denotes the non-negative number whose square is x.

name: Summation
description: Denotes the sum of a sequence of terms. Used with index limits: Σᵢ₌₁ⁿ xᵢ means x₁ + x₂ + ... + xₙ.

name: Product
description: Denotes the product of a sequence of terms. ∏ᵢ₌₁ⁿ xᵢ means x₁ × x₂ × ... × xₙ.

name: Integral
description: The integral sign. ∫f(x)dx denotes the antiderivative or area under the curve f(x). Definite integrals have bounds.

name: Partial Derivative
description: Denotes a partial derivative, used when differentiating a function of multiple variables with respect to one variable while holding others constant.

name: Nabla (Del)
description: The vector differential operator (del/nabla). Used for gradient (∇f), divergence (∇·F), and curl (∇×F) operations.

name: Delta (Uppercase)
description: Uppercase Greek delta. Commonly denotes a change or difference in a quantity (Δx = x₂ - x₁). Also used for the discriminant in the quadratic formula.

name: For All (Universal Quantifier)
description: Universal quantifier in predicate logic. '∀x P(x)' means 'for all x, P(x) is true'.

name: There Exists (Existential Quantifier)
description: Existential quantifier in predicate logic. '∃x P(x)' means 'there exists at least one x such that P(x) is true'.

name: Logical Not (Negation)
description: Logical negation. ¬P is true when P is false, and false when P is true.

name: Logical And (Conjunction)
description: Logical conjunction. P ∧ Q is true only when both P and Q are true.

name: Logical Or (Disjunction)
description: Logical disjunction. P ∨ Q is true when at least one of P or Q is true.

name: Exclusive Or (XOR)
description: Exclusive or (XOR). P ⊕ Q is true when exactly one of P or Q is true (not both). Also used for direct sum in algebra.

name: Element Of
description: Denotes set membership. 'x ∈ S' means x is an element (member) of the set S.

name: Not an Element Of
description: Denotes that an element does not belong to a set. 'x ∉ S' means x is not a member of set S.

name: Proper Subset
description: Strict subset. 'A ⊂ B' means every element of A is in B, and A ≠ B.

name: Subset or Equal
description: Non-strict subset. 'A ⊆ B' means every element of A is also in B (A may equal B).
read_as: is a subset of or equal to

name: Proper Superset
description: Strict superset. 'A ⊃ B' means every element of B is in A, and A ≠ B.

name: Union
description: Set union. 'A ∪ B' is the set of all elements that are in A, in B, or in both.

name: Intersection
description: Set intersection. 'A ∩ B' is the set of elements that are in both A and B.

name: Empty Set
description: The empty set (null set), containing no elements. For any set A: A ∩ ∅ = ∅ and A ∪ ∅ = A.

name: Alpha
description: Lowercase Greek alpha. Commonly used for angles, significance levels in statistics, learning rates in machine learning, and the alpha coefficient.

name: Beta
description: Lowercase Greek beta. Used for regression coefficients, type II error probability in statistics, and beta functions.

name: Gamma
description: Lowercase Greek gamma. Used in physics for the Lorentz factor, in mathematics for the Euler-Mascheroni constant, and in statistics for the gamma distribution.

name: Delta (Lowercase)
description: Lowercase Greek delta. Used in calculus for the epsilon-delta definition of limits, small increments, and Dirac delta function.

name: Epsilon
description: Lowercase Greek epsilon. Used in calculus (ε-δ definitions), error terms in approximations, and machine learning for small learning rates.

name: Theta
description: Lowercase Greek theta. Most commonly used to denote an angle in geometry and trigonometry; also used for parameters in statistics and machine learning.

name: Lambda
description: Lowercase Greek lambda. Used in eigenvalue problems (Ax = λx), Poisson distribution rate, regularization in machine learning, and wavelength in physics.

name: Mu
description: Lowercase Greek mu. Universally used for the mean (expected value) of a distribution, and as the SI prefix for micro (10⁻⁶).

name: Pi
description: Lowercase Greek pi. The mathematical constant equal to the ratio of a circle's circumference to its diameter, approximately 3.14159.

name: Sigma (Lowercase)
description: Lowercase Greek sigma. Used for standard deviation, normal distribution parameter, and stress in physics.

name: Tau
description: Lowercase Greek tau. Equal to 2π ≈ 6.28318. Used in time constants, Kendall's rank correlation coefficient, and topology.

name: Phi (Lowercase)
description: Lowercase Greek phi. Commonly used for the golden ratio (φ ≈ 1.618), angles in polar coordinates, and magnetic flux in physics.

name: Psi (Lowercase)
description: Lowercase Greek psi. Used for wave functions in quantum mechanics, digamma function in mathematics, and angles in geometry.

name: Omega (Lowercase)
description: Lowercase Greek omega. Used for angular frequency in physics (ω = 2πf), roots of unity in algebra, and the smallest infinite ordinal in set theory.

name: Gamma (Uppercase)
description: Uppercase Greek gamma. Represents the Gamma function Γ(n) = (n-1)!, a generalization of factorial to complex numbers.

name: Theta (Uppercase)
description: Uppercase Greek theta. Used in Big-Theta notation for tight asymptotic bounds in algorithm complexity analysis.

name: Lambda (Uppercase)
description: Uppercase Greek lambda. Used in physics for the cosmological constant and in linear algebra for a diagonal matrix of eigenvalues.

name: Pi (Uppercase)
description: Uppercase Greek pi. Used as the product operator Π, analogous to Σ for summation.

name: Phi (Uppercase)
description: Uppercase Greek phi. Represents the cumulative distribution function (CDF) of the standard normal distribution in statistics.

name: Psi (Uppercase)
description: Uppercase Greek psi. Used for many-body wave functions in quantum mechanics and the reciprocal Fibonacci constant.

name: Omega (Uppercase)
description: Uppercase Greek omega. Used for ohms (unit of electrical resistance), sample space in probability, and Big-Omega complexity notation.

name: Implies
description: Logical implication (if...then). 'P ⟹ Q' means: if P is true, then Q must also be true.

name: If and Only If
description: Biconditional (if and only if). 'P ⟺ Q' means P and Q are either both true or both false; they are logically equivalent.

name: Therefore
description: Used in proofs and logical arguments to introduce a conclusion that follows from preceding statements.

name: Because
description: Used in mathematical arguments to introduce a reason or justification for a preceding statement.

name: Perpendicular
description: Indicates that two lines, planes, or vectors are perpendicular (at right angles to each other). Also used for logical falsehood (bottom).

name: Parallel
description: Indicates that two lines or planes are parallel (never intersect and are always equidistant).

name: Angle
description: Denotes an angle formed by two rays. '∠ABC' refers to the angle at vertex B formed by rays BA and BC.

name: Degree
description: Unit of angular measure. One full rotation is 360°. Also used for temperature in Celsius and Fahrenheit.

name: Factorial
description: Factorial operator. n! = n × (n-1) × ... × 2 × 1. By convention, 0! = 1. Used in combinatorics and series expansions.

name: Absolute Value
description: The absolute value (modulus) of x: the non-negative value of x regardless of sign. |x| = x if x ≥ 0, and -x if x < 0.

name: Floor
description: The floor function returns the greatest integer less than or equal to x. ⌊3.7⌋ = 3, ⌊-1.2⌋ = -2.

name: Ceiling
description: The ceiling function returns the smallest integer greater than or equal to x. ⌈3.2⌉ = 4, ⌈-1.7⌉ = -1.

name: Percent
description: Denotes a proportion out of 100. 45% = 45/100 = 0.45. Also used as the modulo operator in programming languages.
